title: OIDC Trust Regression Exposed Analytics Exporter Token
summary: A suspicious Customer Records export spike followed an IAM trust-policy change that widened GitHub OIDC access from the protected main branch to a repo-wide wildcard. Specialist agents must decide what was accessed, whether the token is contained, and which irreversible decisions require human approval.

notes: This packet is synthetic lab evidence using real-world log shapes.
IP addresses use documentation ranges and should not be treated as real attribution.
Token-like values are redacted labels only and are not usable credentials.

evidence.description: Customer Records API gateway request logs around the OIDC-issued token abuse.
Federated-token issuance, service-token use, and revocation events.
CloudTrail-shaped IAM trust-policy and STS events.
IAM policy diff that widened GitHub OIDC trust to unprotected refs.
Secret scanner output showing this was not a committed-secret incident.
Indicator notes for source IPs seen in the OIDC abuse window.
Human-approval and customer-notification policy used by Risk & Compliance.
